compiled_state.title: test-behavioral-equivalence-allowlist.sh times out causing test-batched.sh and run-all.sh to stall
compiled_state.author: Test

comments.body:
## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] test-behavioral-equivalence-allowlist.sh completes within 60 seconds
- [ ] Verify: timeout 60 bash tests/scripts/test-behavioral-equivalence-allowlist.sh; test $? -ne 124
- [ ] bash tests/run-all.sh no longer stalls on this test
comments.author: Test

comments.body: CHECKPOINT: Fix applied. Root cause: compute_diff_hash_excludes_file() called bash compute-diff-hash.sh twice per file (hash_before + hash_after), for a total of ~20 subprocess invocations across 10 tests. Each cold-path invocation was slow (~3-7s) due to (a) config-paths.sh spawning 6 read-config.sh subprocesses, (b) checkpoint walk (10 git log + rev-parse calls), and (c) no cache hits since staging/unstaging changes the index mtime between calls. Fix: (1) pre-compute BASELINE_HASH once after git init, reuse across all tests; (2) export WORKFLOW_CONFIG_FILE to skip git-based config resolution in each call; (3) export WORKFLOW_PLUGIN_ARTIFACTS_DIR to skip get_artifacts_dir overhead. Result: test now completes in ~25s (down from >73s timeout).
comments.author: Test

comments.body:
CHECKPOINT COMPLETE: Two fixes applied to tests/hooks/test-behavioral-equivalence-allowlist.sh:

1. BASELINE_HASH pre-computation: Moved hash_before computation outside the per-test helper. Was: 1 bash subprocess per file for hash_before × 10-12 tests = 10-12 extra invocations. Now: computed once after git init.

2. Cache collision fix: compute-diff-hash.sh caches by git index mtime (seconds precision). When multiple tests run in <1s, consecutive index staging/unstaging operations share the same mtime → stale cache hits → wrong hashes returned. Fix: pass TMPDIR=<call-unique-dir> for each compute-diff-hash.sh invocation, giving each call an empty cache namespace.

3. WORKFLOW_CONFIG_FILE + WORKFLOW_PLUGIN_ARTIFACTS_DIR: Set at init time to skip git-based config resolution and artifact dir discovery overhead in each invocation.

Result: Test now runs in ~8-26s (consistently) vs. >73s timeout. All 39 assertions pass in 6/6 consecutive runs.
comments.author: Test

comments.body: Fixed: completed as part of batch 5
comments.author: Test
